{{Fig|After forgetting, the optimum new interval is usually very short (less than 4 days). It depends on the number of memory lapses (for many lapses it is nearly always one day). It also depends on the [[Glossary:Retrievability|retrievability]] at the moment of forgetting (high retrievability usually indicates fresh repetitions). In the presented example, the user runs the approximation procedure to optimally fit the post-stability surface to recorded data. In the presented case, the formula is <code>Stability{{=}}0.6+3.5*Power(R,1.8)*Exp(-0.27*Laps)</code> (see: [https://claude.ai/public/artifacts/443569dd-7f93-4706-8c9a-894c26a8e4e8 visualization])}}
